name: The Rift Awakening
subtitle: Four strangers, one fractured world
location: The Summoning Grounds to Sacred Ruins of Aethoria

shard.name: Seal Fragment of Origin
shard.description: A pale white crystal that pulses like a heartbeat. The echo of a world that once was whole.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The Sacred Ruins of Aethoria. What was once a place of light and ceremony is now a hollow throne of shadow. And on that throne — something waits.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: It was a warrior once. A name. A life. Valdris took both and left only the shape of a soldier, filled with darkness and given one purpose: guard the Seal Fragment. Forever.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
pre_dialogue.text: I can feel what it lost. There is no malice in it. Only obedience. That makes it more dangerous, not less.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
pre_dialogue.text: ...I have guided many souls onward. I have never seen one forced to stay like this. This is not death. This is something worse.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
pre_dialogue.text: Can we free it? After we —

pre_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
pre_dialogue.text: We defeat it first. Then we ask that question.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The Void Knight raises its blade. It does not speak. It has forgotten how. The only thing left in it is the order it was given — and the four of them stand between it and that order.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The Void Knight staggers. The darkness holding it together begins to fracture — hairline cracks of pale light splitting through the shadow armor.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: There. Do you see it? Underneath all that shadow — there is still something in there.

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: A remnant. It has been in there the whole time.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The Void Knight goes still. The blade lowers. And for one brief, impossible moment — something like recognition crosses what remains of its face.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: Then the shadow collapses inward. The knight crumbles. And where it stood, a pale crystal pulses once — like a heartbeat — and goes quiet. The first Seal Fragment.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: One fragment. Four to go. We honour what it was by finishing this.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: I will remember its name. When we find out what it was — I will remember.

scenes.narration: Silence. Then a tearing of the world. The sound of something ancient waking. A figure stands alone in cracked stone ruins — not fallen, but arriving with the gravity of a stone dropped from heaven.

Xiao's eyes snap open. This place is not Liyue. The air tastes of old magic and dying light. His hand reaches automatically for the amber glow that should respond to his call — the power that has sustained him for two thousand years.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: ...The Vision. It does not...

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Your world is distant now, Yaksha. The power you carried belongs to Teyvat. Here, you carry something else.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: Who speaks? Show yourself.

scenes.narration: There is no figure. Only the ruins, and the strange purple luminescence that settles over them like twilight that never came before. Xiao reaches inward, past the familiar emptiness where his Vision should be, and finds something else awakening — deeper, older, more attuned to this wounded world.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: Karmic resonance. This world suffers as I do. Something broke here — something fundamental.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: You were called because you alone carry the weight to stand against it. Your burden in Teyvat has become your strength in Aethoria.

scenes.narration: Power thrums through him — not the familiar elemental clarity of Anemo, but something rawer. The karmic contamination that has plagued him for millennia suddenly makes sense here. It is not a curse in this place. It is a compass.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: Others have been called as well. I can sense it. Multiple presences, each touching down where the light is brightest.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Four summoned. Four anchors for a world falling apart. Will you stand?

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: ...I have fought alone for two thousand years. It would be dishonest to say I am unwilling to try otherwise.

scenes.narration: Light tears through existence. A figure falls through the break in worlds with the precision of training — rolling, rising, sword drawn in a single motion. Ayaka lands in a crouch on ancient stone, blade gleaming with frost that will not melt.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: ...This is not Inazuma. Not any place in Teyvat I have been taught to recognize.

scenes.narration: She reaches for her Vision — the Cryo gem that has always answered her call, that has made her graceful strikes possible, that has been as much a part of her as her name. Her hand closes on emptiness.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: The Vision. It does not respond.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: What you carried was born from your world. In this world, you are born anew.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: You speak as though you know me. Yet I know nothing of this place, and less of you.

scenes.narration: She does not panic. Instead, she stands, closing her eyes. Her brother taught her that composure is not the absence of feeling — it is the mastery of it. She breathes, and in that breathing, finds something else awakening within her. A connection to this place's pain. A pull toward something that needs to be done.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: There is something... broken. A world being unmade. And I have been called to help mend it.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: You carry the weight of legacy. Here, that weight becomes purpose.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: Purpose I can bear. My clan has always answered when duty called. This is merely a different duty.

scenes.narration: Something stirs within her — not the sharp cryo magic of her Vision, but something broader. An ability to sense what this world needs from her, independent of her own power. A clarity that requires no gem to manifest.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: I am not alone here. There are others. I can feel them arriving, one by one.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Four will meet in the ruins. Four will be needed to hold back the shadow.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: Then I will stand with them. Whatever comes.

scenes.narration: Water cascades through a rift in the world. Nilou emerges not with violence but with grace, her dancer's instincts translating the chaos into something resembling a step. She lands on fractured stone, and the first thing she notices is the stars.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: The constellations... they are wrong. Or perhaps I have never seen them at all.

scenes.narration: She reaches for the familiar coolness of her water Vision, seeking the connection to the sacred pools of Sumeru. For a moment, there is only silence. Then panic — small, carefully hidden behind a dancer's smile.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: Where is the water? Where are my waters?

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Your vision belonged to Teyvat. Here, you must learn to hear a new song.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: I do not know this place. I do not know its song.

scenes.narration: But as she stands there, surrounded by ruin and strangeness, something begins to come clear. Not a song she recognizes, but a rhythm. A heartbeat underneath the broken stone. The world itself bleeding, calling out, needing something to answer its tempo.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: This world is crying. I can hear it. Not in words, but... in feeling. In the spaces between things.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Your gift was always to feel beauty in the temporary. This world is temporary. Broken. Will you dance with it?

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: ...Even broken things have beauty. Even dying things deserve to be heard.

scenes.narration: She does not move like a warrior. She moves like a dancer — and yet every motion carries purpose now, synchronized with the world's need. Her new power is not the sharp clarity of water magic, but the intuitive understanding of what this place feels.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: There are others coming. Three more to join this broken world's song.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Four called to Aethoria. Four to dance with its final hope.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: Then I will teach them the steps.

scenes.narration: A burst of flame, then laughter. Hu Tao tumbles through a rift in the world like it is the most natural thing, landing on broken stone with a grin that does not quite reach her eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Well! This is far more interesting than yesterday's paperwork~ Where am I this time?

scenes.narration: She reaches for her Vision — the Pyro gem that has burned bright since she was young, the power that connected her to the spirit realm and all the souls that needed guidance. For a moment, her hand grips at nothing, and the smile falters.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: The flame. It will not answer.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Your world's fire burns no longer. But you have been called to this world's dying light.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Dying? Now that is poetic. And troubling. I do not usually guide the living.

scenes.narration: She steadies herself, straightens her coat, and forces the smile back. But beneath it, something deeper is stirring — a sensitivity to the boundary between life and death in this place. She can feel it now: a world at its ending, souls trapped in transition, a silence that should not be so quiet.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: This world is dying. Not quickly. Not naturally. Someone is holding it here, letting it waste away piece by piece.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: You see truly. And yet you were summoned not to mourn it, but to save it.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: A world worth saving, then. That changes things.

scenes.narration: Power ignites within her — not the familiar warmth of her Pyro Vision, but something deeper. An understanding of cycles, of endings, of when to let go and when to fight to hold on. Her gift is no longer to guide the dead — it is to understand what is worth saving from death.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Three others are being summoned. I can feel them — each one a voice in this dying world's chorus.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Four to answer. Four to choose what Aethoria will become.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Then let us make sure it is a choice worth making.

scenes.narration: Silence. Then light — violent, white, all-consuming. Then the sound of breathing that is not one's own.
Ayaka lands on cracked stone, sword already drawn by instinct. The ruins around her are ancient and broken. This is not Inazuma.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: ...Where am I? This architecture resembles nothing in Teyvat records.

scenes.narration: A burst of flame ignites nearby as Hu Tao tumbles through a crackling rift, already grinning.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Ohoho~! Now THIS is the most interesting thing that has happened to me all week, and I was chasing a malevolent spirit this morning!

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: ...You seem remarkably calm for someone who just fell out of thin air.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Funeral directors must stay composed around the unexpected! Also this is exciting, not scary. Very important distinction.

scenes.narration: Water cascades from a second rift as Nilou steps through, her dancer instincts keeping her upright.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: Oh... the stars here are a different color. Did we cross worlds?

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: The boundary between worlds is thinner than people think. I have always suspected as much.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: Whatever brought us here, we should assess before assuming we are safe.

scenes.narration: He does not fall through the rift. He steps through it. Xiao surveys the ruins with eyes that have seen the end of things before.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: This place reeks of karmic contamination. Something ancient has been wounded here.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: You can sense that? Are you an adeptus?

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: I am the last yaksha. I carry enough karma to recognize when a world is dying. This world is dying.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: A world on the edge of passing, and something called us here rather than letting it go quietly. Rude. I am intrigued.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The ruins are not empty. Shadow creatures lurk among the broken columns, drawn to the light of the summoning.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
pre_dialogue.text: Shadow-touched. They serve the Emperor. End them.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
pre_dialogue.text: I was just starting to feel restless! Let us go!

post_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
post_dialogue.text: We work well together. Considering we just met.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: I am Kamisato Ayaka. I am glad to have met warriors of your caliber.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: Hu Tao, 77th Director of Wangsheng Funeral Parlor! Amateur poet! Very pleased!

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: Xiao. We can trade names later. We are being watched.

scenes.narration: At the heart of the ruins stands a figure barely corporeal, wrapped in flickering light. The Oracle of Aethoria.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Oracle: I called you from the furthest reaches of existence. Four who carry the resonance of the broken Seals. Forgive me — I had no right. And yet I had no choice.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: Explain.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Oracle: The Shadow Emperor Valdris has shattered four of the Five Elemental Seals. When the final Seal breaks, every world connected to Aethoria will be consumed — including yours.

scenes.narration: Ayaka tightens her grip. Nilou catches her breath. Hutao tilts her head with professional interest. Xiao closes his eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: If our worlds are at risk, we have no choice but to act. What must be done?

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Oracle: Valdris draws power from four Seal Fragments scattered as anchors across Aethoria. Destroy them, and he will be weak enough to face directly.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: A scavenger hunt for ancient doom-crystals. Much more interesting than the average Tuesday.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: Tell us where to go. We will handle the rest.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Oracle: The first Fragment lies in the Sacred Ruins, guarded by the Void Knight — a warrior Valdris unmade and rebuilt in shadow.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: It never does.

scenes.narration: As they turn to leave, a cold stillness descends over the ruins. The air pressure drops. The shadows around the chamber's edge lengthen — inward, toward the four of them — without any source of light changing.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: Something is watching.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: ...I feel it too. Something very, very old.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: It is not the Oracle.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: No. It is not.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: No voice. No form. Only the absolute, bone-deep certainty that whatever waits at the end of this road already knows their names. Has always known their names. And is in no hurry at all.
